title: TextFormat::Parser null-dereferences default error collector on oversize input

direct_impact.operation: read/write through null error collector pointer during oversized-input error handling

chain_potential.standalone: process crash and denial of service in services that parse attacker-controlled textproto larger than INT_MAX
chain_potential.with_other_bugs: limited; primarily useful as a crash primitive or reliability amplifier in larger resource-exhaustion attacks

justification.AV: N - Reachable through network-facing services that accept untrusted textproto input.
justification.AC: H - Requires a payload larger than 2147483647 bytes, which is an atypically large precondition.
justification.PR: N - No privileges are required to send the malicious input.
justification.UI: N - The vulnerable parser crashes during automatic processing.
justification.S: U - Impact is limited to the vulnerable process or service.
justification.C: N - No confidentiality impact was demonstrated.
justification.I: N - No integrity impact was demonstrated.
justification.A: H - The confirmed result is a deterministic process crash.

notes: LLDB evidence confirms x19 == 0x0 and error_collector_ == nullptr at the crash site.
Scope is availability-only because no memory corruption or data disclosure was observed.
